tool_args.label: Metro-North Railroad Financial and Ridership Reports – January 2023

warnings: source.publisher is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Publisher has 39 accepted/canonical observations and should link to entity context when useful. Suggested relation: published_by.

payload.description: MTA commuter railroad serving East of Hudson lines
Total revenue of $57.9 million was $6.2 million lower than the Adopted Budget
Through January 2023 ridership was 34.8% below 2019 pre-COVID levels (adjusted for the same number of workdays)
Farebox revenue of $41.2 million was $3.7 million higher than the Budget
At the end of January, total headcount was 6,288
Total headcount was 340 lower than the Budget of 6,628

tool_args.label: Total Expenses before Non-Cash Liability Adjustments

payload.description: Total expenses before non-cash liability adjustments of $138.4 million were $9.9 million or 6.7% favorable to the Budget
Commutation ridership of 1.9 million was 144.3% above 2022 and 20.9% above the Budget
Non-Commutation ridership of 2.6 million was 58.5% above 2022 and 4.4% above the Budget

tool_args.label: Adjusted Farebox Operating Ratio January YTD

payload.description: The year-to-date January Adjusted Farebox Operating Ratio was 37.5%

tool_args.label: Adjusted Cost per Passenger January YTD

payload.description: The year-to-date January Adjusted Cost per Passenger was $25.65
The year-to-date January Revenue per Passenger was $9.20

tool_args.label: January Commutation Farebox Revenue vs Budget
January Non-Commutation Farebox Revenue vs Budget
January Total Farebox Revenue vs Budget

payload.description: Standard Farebox Operating Ratio for January 2023
Adjusted Farebox Operating Ratio for January 2023

payload.claim_text: Farebox Revenues were $3.7 million favorable to the Budget due to increased ridership volumes across all East of Hudson lines
payload.subject: Farebox Revenue

payload.claim_text: Other Operating Revenues were $2.6 million unfavorable to the Budget due primarily to lower Grand Central Terminal (GCT) net retail revenue
payload.subject: Other Operating Revenue

payload.claim_text: Total expenses before non-cash liability adjustments of $138.4 million were $9.9 million or 6.7% favorable to the Budget. The primary drivers include the timing of capital expenditures and maintenance contracts as well as vacant positions partially offset by higher overtime costs
payload.subject: Total Expenses

payload.gap_text: Results are based on the preliminary close of the general ledger and are subject to review and adjustment
payload.missing_information: Final adjusted results are not yet available
payload.description: January 2023 financial results are preliminary and subject to audit review and revision
Net Cash Surplus/(Deficit) from Cash Conversion
